title: Communications and Digital Engagement Officer at UNITEE (Brussels, Belgium): Apply for a European Communications Career Supporting Entrepreneurship, Inclusion, and EU Projects
summary: If you are passionate about communications, digital storytelling, European cooperation, entrepreneurship, and creating meaningful social impact, the Communications and Digital Engagement Officer position at UNITEE – New European Business Confederation offers an outstanding opportunity to begin or advance your career in the heart of Europe. Based in Brussels, Belgium , this full-time role is designed for an early-career communications professional who enjoys transforming complex ideas into engaging digital content while supporting European projects focused on entrepreneurship, diversity, inclusion, vocational education, and social innovation. Successful candidates will work alongside an international team, contribute to European advocacy initiatives, support digital campaigns, organize events, and create compelling multimedia content that reaches stakeholders across Europe. Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026, 18:00 CET. About UNITEE UNITEE (New European Business Confederation) is a European network representing entrepreneurs and business professionals from diverse cultural backgrounds. The organization collaborates with: National business federations Business support organizations Civil society organizations Academic institutions Public authorities European partners UNITEE works to strengthen entrepreneurship ecosystems by promoting: Inclusive entrepreneurship Sustainable economic growth Diversity, equity and inclusion Innovation European cooperation Equal business opportunities Through advocacy, communication campaigns, networking, coaching, and EU-funded projects, UNITEE helps entrepreneurs from underrepresented communities establish, grow, and expand successful businesses across Europe. About the Communications and Digital Engagement Officer Position UNITEE is recruiting a Communications and Digital Engagement Officer to join its Brussels office beginning in September 2026 . This position supports communication and dissemination activities across various European initiatives while helping strengthen UNITEE s digital presence and organizational visibility. The role is ideal for someone who enjoys: Storytelling Graphic design Website management Social media Digital campaigns Event communications European affairs Content creation Applicants do not need to satisfy every listed requirement. UNITEE welcomes candidates with different experiences who demonstrate enthusiasm, creativity, willingness to learn, and a commitment to inclusion. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will support communication activities across several areas. 1. Communications and Content Creation Responsibilities include: Planning communication campaigns Supporting dissemination activities for advocacy and European projects Creating engaging social media content Writing articles Developing newsletters Producing digital campaigns Designing presentations Creating promotional materials Producing success stories Preparing communication reports Monitoring campaign performance Creating editorial calendars Simplifying complex European policy topics into accessible content 2. Website and Digital Platform Management The Communications Officer will also: Update UNITEE project websites Work with WordPress Use Elementor Publish: News Events Publications Project results Improve website usability Maintain consistent branding Support online visibility campaigns Develop digital resources 3. Design and Multimedia Production A major component of the role focuses on visual communication. The successful candidate will create: Brochures Infographics Reports Presentations Social media graphics Event materials Promotional assets Videos Podcasts Interviews They will also: Develop design templates Maintain brand consistency Follow emerging communication and design trends 4. Events and Stakeholder Engagement The officer will support numerous European events, including: Conferences Workshops Webinars Networking events Roundtables Responsibilities include: Preparing event materials Developing participant information Supporting event promotion Managing communication before, during and after events Creating visibility materials Educational Requirements Applicants should possess a degree in one of the following fields: Communications Marketing Journalism European Studies Related disciplines Professional Experience Required Candidates should have: At least one year of relevant experience Experience may come from: Professional employment Internships Volunteering Community projects Student organizations NGO work Experience with: NGOs European organizations EU-funded projects is considered an advantage. Required Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Excellent written English Excellent spoken English Strong visual communication skills Creativity Good organizational skills Attention to detail Social media communication experience Content creation skills Canva experience Adobe Express experience (or similar tools) Familiarity with newsletter platforms Email marketing tools Personal Qualities UNITEE Values UNITEE is looking for candidates who are: Curious Creative Proactive Flexible Respectful Collaborative Independent Passionate about European cooperation Interested in diversity and inclusion Enthusiastic about entrepreneurship Motivated to keep learning Portfolio Encouraged Applicants are encouraged to include examples of work such as: Graphic design Social media campaigns Videos Websites Presentations Marketing materials Communication portfolios Benefits of Working at UNITEE Successful candidates will receive: One-year fixed-term Belgian employment contract (CDD) Possibility of contract renewal Competitive salary appropriate for an early-career professional Reimbursement of Brussels public transport (STIB/MIVB pass) Exposure to European Union-funded projects Opportunities to work on advocacy initiatives Experience in European communications Multicultural working environment Professional growth opportunities Networking with European stakeholders Practical experience in communications, digital engagement, and policy dissemination Why This Opportunity Stands Out This role provides significantly more than a traditional communications position. Employees will gain practical experience in: European project communications EU advocacy Policy dissemination Digital storytelling Website management Event communications International stakeholder engagement Multimedia production Inclusive entrepreneurship initiatives It offers an excellent pathway into European institutions, international NGOs, communications agencies, and public policy organizations. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit: An updated CV A short motivation letter Applications should be emailed to: info@unitee.eu Use the subject line: APPLICATION: Communication and Digital Engagement Officer Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as UNITEE reviews applications on a rolling basis and may close recruitment before the advertised deadline if a suitable candidate is identified. Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026, 18:00 CET Work Location Brussels, Belgium Expected start date: September 2026 Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about communications, digital engagement, European cooperation, entrepreneurship, and social impact, this position offers an excellent opportunity to build your career while contributing to projects that promote diversity, inclusion, innovation, and sustainable economic development across Europe. Working at UNITEE provides valuable international exposure, practical communications experience, and the chance to collaborate with experts, policymakers, entrepreneurs, and organizations from across the European Union. For more information visit the official website Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Communications and Digital Engagement Officer at UNITEE (Brussels, Belgium): Apply for a European Communications Career Supporting Entrepreneurship, Inclusion, and EU Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Taylor’s University Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business 2026: Complete Guide to Admission, Fees, Duration, Research Areas, Scholarships & Career Opportunities
summary: Pursuing a Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business is one of the highest academic achievements for professionals, researchers, educators, and future industry leaders seeking to contribute meaningful knowledge to the global business landscape. Taylor s University, one of Malaysia s leading private universities, offers a comprehensive PhD in Business programme that equips candidates with advanced research capabilities while allowing them to investigate real-world business challenges. Whether your interests lie in entrepreneurship, finance, marketing, supply chain management, leadership, or human capital development, this doctoral programme provides an excellent platform to develop innovative solutions that influence academia, industry, and public policy. Applications are currently open across multiple intakes in 2026, giving prospective candidates flexibility in planning their doctoral journey. Why Study the PhD in Business at Taylor s University? Taylor s University has established itself as one of Asia s leading higher education institutions, known for academic excellence, impactful research, and strong industry collaboration. The Doctor of Philosophy in Business is specifically designed for individuals who wish to: Develop advanced research competencies. Produce original contributions to business knowledge. Address contemporary business and economic challenges. Work closely with internationally recognised academics. Build expertise that supports careers in academia, consulting, policymaking, entrepreneurship, and executive leadership. Unlike coursework-based postgraduate degrees, this programme focuses extensively on independent research guided by experienced supervisors. Programme Overview The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business allows students to conduct rigorous research across numerous business disciplines while developing strong analytical, methodological, and critical thinking skills. Students have the opportunity to investigate topics including: Entrepreneurship Human Capital Development Financial Economics Investment Marketing Supply Chain Management Managerial Leadership Business Innovation Strategic Management Organisational Behaviour Corporate Governance International Business Business Sustainability Throughout the programme, students are encouraged to generate original knowledge that contributes to academic literature while offering practical solutions to business challenges. Because Taylor s prioritises quality supervision, only a limited number of doctoral candidates are admitted during each intake, allowing supervisors to provide personalised academic guidance. Research Environment One of the strongest advantages of the programme is its research-focused learning environment. Students benefit from: Supervision by experienced academics Access to internationally recognised researchers Collaboration opportunities Research seminars Academic workshops Professional networking Publication support Access to modern research facilities Taylor s also highlights that many of its academics are recognised among the Stanford World s Top 2% Scientists , offering students valuable mentorship throughout their doctoral studies. Programme Duration The programme offers flexible study options suitable for both working professionals and full-time researchers. Full-Time 3 to 6 years Part-Time 4 to 8 years This flexibility enables candidates to balance research with professional or personal commitments. Study Modes Applicants may choose from: Full-Time Part-Time The programme also offers: Normal Mode Prior Publication Mode This flexibility accommodates candidates with different research backgrounds and academic goals. 2026 Intake Months Taylor s University offers several admission opportunities throughout the year. Available intakes include: February April June September October Since admissions are selective, prospective applicants are encouraged to apply early. Tuition Fees The published tuition fees for 2026 are: Local Students Normal Mode MYR 51,560 Prior Publication Mode MYR 34,640 International Students Normal Mode USD 18,474 Prior Publication Mode USD 13,589 Applicants should note: Fees are reviewed annually. Effective 1 July 2025, a 6% Service Tax applies to non-Malaysian citizens on applicable fees (excluding International Security Deposit and EMGS application fees). Scholarships and Financial Support Taylor s University encourages prospective doctoral students to explore available scholarship opportunities and financial aid schemes that may help reduce the cost of study. Applicants are advised to review the university s scholarship offerings before applying. Why Choose Taylor s University for Your PhD? Several factors distinguish Taylor s PhD in Business from many doctoral programmes. These include: 1. Research Excellence Students conduct original research addressing contemporary business challenges. 2. Expert Academic Supervision Candidates receive guidance from internationally recognised scholars. 3. Flexible Learning Both full-time and part-time options are available. 4. Multiple Annual Intakes Five admission periods provide flexibility for applicants. 5. Strong Research Community Students collaborate with fellow researchers and industry experts. 6. Career Development Graduates gain advanced research, analytical, leadership, and academic skills. Admission Process Taylor s University has streamlined the application process for prospective doctoral candidates. Applicants should: Review admission requirements. Prepare all required academic documents. Submit an online application. Provide any additional documentation requested. Await confirmation from the admissions office. The university provides comprehensive admissions guidance through its official admissions portal. Who Should Apply? This programme is ideal for: University lecturers Academic researchers Business professionals Consultants Policy analysts Entrepreneurs Corporate executives Government officers NGO professionals Individuals seeking careers in research or higher education Candidates with strong research interests and a passion for advancing business knowledge will particularly benefit from the programme. Career Opportunities Graduates of the Doctor of Philosophy in Business can pursue careers across academia, government, multinational corporations, consulting firms, research institutions, and international organisations. Potential career pathways include: University Professor Lecturer Senior Research Fellow Business Consultant Corporate Strategist Policy Researcher Economist Executive Director Innovation Manager Entrepreneurship Advisor Business Development Director Research and Development Manager Think Tank Researcher The advanced research skills acquired throughout the programme are highly valued across numerous industries. Academic Experience at Taylor s University Taylor s University emphasises an ecosystem that nurtures intellectual development, creativity, and practical wisdom. Students benefit from: Collaborative learning Industry engagement Alumni networking Academic conferences Research seminars Cross-disciplinary collaboration Modern campus facilities This environment supports both academic excellence and professional growth. Important Information Applicants should note: Limited doctoral places are available each intake. Early application is highly recommended. Tuition fees are subject to annual review. International students may be subject to additional government-related charges and taxes. Learn more and Apply Here Final Thoughts Taylor s University continues to position itself as a leading destination for postgraduate research in Asia. Its Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business programme offers aspiring researchers the opportunity to investigate critical business issues, contribute original scholarship, and work alongside internationally recognised academics. With flexible study options, multiple annual intakes, comprehensive research support, and access to world-class supervision, this doctoral programme provides an excellent pathway for individuals seeking careers in academia, research, policy, or executive leadership. Prospective candidates interested in shaping the future of business through rigorous research should consider applying early to secure a place in this competitive programme. For more information, visit the official website Discover global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Taylor’s University Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business 2026: Complete Guide to Admission, Fees, Duration, Research Areas, Scholarships Career Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) 2026: Fully Funded ECOWAS Fellowship for Young Women Leaders Driving Climate Mobility Solutions
summary: The Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) 2026 is now accepting applications from outstanding young women across the ECOWAS region who are passionate about climate action, migration, gender equality, and community development. This prestigious six-month regional fellowship is designed to strengthen women’s leadership, build technical capacity, and support community-led solutions addressing the growing challenge of climate-induced human mobility in West Africa. Implemented by the Women Environmental Programme (WEP) under the GIZ-led Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) Programme, the fellowship equips emerging women leaders with the knowledge, mentorship, funding, and regional networks needed to influence climate and migration policies while implementing impactful community projects. Supported by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH , co-financed by the Swiss Development Cooperation (SDC) and commissioned by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) , this fellowship provides a unique opportunity for women to become influential advocates for gender-responsive climate resilience and sustainable development throughout the ECOWAS region. About the Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) Climate change is increasingly forcing people across West Africa to migrate due to drought, flooding, desertification, coastal erosion, and other environmental pressures. Women and girls are among those most affected because of existing social, economic, and gender inequalities. Despite playing vital roles in building community resilience, women remain significantly underrepresented in climate migration governance, policy formulation, and decision-making processes. The Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) was established to address this gap by empowering young women leaders with practical skills, leadership training, mentorship, financial support, and regional collaboration opportunities. The programme enables participants to: Strengthen their leadership capacity. Influence climate mobility policies. Develop community-driven solutions. Build regional partnerships. Participate in national, regional, and international climate and migration dialogues. Fellowship Objectives The fellowship has been designed to achieve several important objectives across the ECOWAS region. 1. Strengthen Women s Leadership The programme seeks to increase women s leadership and participation in climate mobility governance and policy processes. 2. Build Capacity Participants will receive specialized knowledge and practical skills needed to address climate-induced migration using gender-responsive approaches. 3. Support Community-Based Solutions Fellows will receive support to design and implement pilot projects that respond directly to climate mobility challenges affecting their communities. 4. Promote Regional Collaboration The programme creates opportunities for peer learning, networking, and collaboration among young women leaders from different ECOWAS countries. 5. Increase Women s Representation The fellowship aims to ensure more women actively contribute to regional and global discussions on climate change, migration, and sustainable development. Fellowship Duration The programme runs for: Duration: Six months Programme Period: July–December 2026 Fellowship Format The fellowship combines both virtual and in-person learning experiences through a hybrid model that includes: Virtual orientation Fully funded in-person training Individual mentorship Community project implementation Regional networking activities Fellowship Structure The fellowship is delivered in three comprehensive phases. Phase One: Recruitment, Orientation and Capacity Building Selected fellows will participate in a fully funded training programme covering key topics such as: Climate mobility in West Africa Gender mainstreaming in climate and migration policy Policy engagement and advocacy Leadership development Community impact strategies Training sessions will be facilitated by experienced regional experts and mentors. To ensure inclusivity, all sessions will include: Simultaneous English and French interpretation Bilingual learning materials Phase Two: Mentorship and Pilot Project Implementation Individual Mentorship Each fellow will be matched with an experienced mentor based on: Thematic interests Professional background Preferred language Participants will receive monthly coaching and technical guidance throughout the programme. Pilot Project Grant A major highlight of the fellowship is access to financial support for community initiatives. Eligible fellows may receive grants ranging from: USD 500–USD 1,000 The funding supports pilot projects or research initiatives focusing on climate-related human mobility. Examples include: Gender-responsive climate mobility research Community climate resilience initiatives Public awareness campaigns Policy advocacy projects Evidence-generation activities Livelihood programmes Capacity-building initiatives for vulnerable communities Phase Three: Regional and International Participation Outstanding fellows may receive support to participate in: Regional policy dialogues International climate conferences Migration forums Gender equality events Advocacy platforms Support may include: Pre-event coaching Policy brief development Networking opportunities Interpretation services Language support Community of Practice and Alumni Network Upon completing the fellowship, participants automatically become members of the regional Community of Practice and Alumni Network. Benefits include: Continued peer learning Quarterly knowledge-sharing sessions Professional networking Regional collaboration opportunities Access to future climate and development opportunities Language Support To encourage participation from across the ECOWAS region, all fellowship activities are conducted bilingually. Participants receive: English interpretation French interpretation Professionally translated learning materials This enables meaningful cross-cultural collaboration among fellows from different linguistic backgrounds. Fellowship Benefits Successful applicants will enjoy numerous benefits throughout the programme, including: Fully funded leadership and capacity-building training Hybrid learning experience Expert mentorship Monthly coaching sessions Community pilot project grants worth USD 500–1,000 Regional networking opportunities Participation in policy dialogues Access to international advocacy events (for outstanding fellows) Membership in a regional alumni network Long-term collaboration opportunities English and French interpretation support Exposure to regional climate and migration experts Eligible Pilot Project Areas Applicants are encouraged to propose projects focusing on areas such as: Climate-induced human mobility Community resilience Gender-responsive climate action Environmental sustainability Migration research Community awareness campaigns Livelihood development Policy advocacy Evidence generation Capacity building for vulnerable populations Eligibility Criteria The fellowship welcomes applications from young women aged 18–35 years who are nationals of ECOWAS Member States and are passionate about climate action and community development. Applicants should: Demonstrate leadership experience in climate change, migration, environmental sustainability, gender advocacy, youth engagement, or community development. Be actively involved in community initiatives, civil society organizations, research institutions, or policy advocacy. Have a project idea or ongoing initiative related to climate-induced human mobility. Show commitment to policy influence and community impact. Be available for the full duration of the fellowship. Possess good communication skills. Be willing to collaborate across cultures and languages. Eligible Countries The fellowship will recruit young women leaders from ECOWAS Member States, including: Anglophone Countries Nigeria Ghana Gambia Liberia Sierra Leone Francophone Countries Benin Côte d’Ivoire Guinea Senegal Togo Lusophone Countries Cape Verde Guinea-Bissau Application Requirements Applicants must compile the following documents into a single PDF or Word file: 1. Motivation Letter Maximum one page explaining: Interest in the fellowship Career goals Alignment with fellowship objectives 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) Maximum two pages highlighting: Relevant experience Leadership achievements Community involvement 3. Project Proposal Maximum three pages describing: The proposed project or initiative Expected impact How it will be developed during the fellowship Application Submission Applications should be submitted via email to: Fellowship.HMCCC@wepnigeria.net Applicants should use the following subject format: HMCCC Fellowship Application – Applicant Name – Country Example: HMCCC Fellowship Application – Amina Diallo – Senegal Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 19 July 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their documents early and submit well before the deadline. Why You Should Apply The Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change offers much more than leadership training. It provides a unique platform for young women to become influential advocates for climate resilience and gender equality across West Africa. Participants will: Strengthen their leadership skills. Gain practical knowledge in climate mobility. Receive mentorship from experienced professionals. Access funding for community projects. Expand regional and international networks. Influence climate and migration policies. Join a long-term alumni community dedicated to sustainable development. For women passionate about climate justice, migration policy, environmental sustainability, and community leadership, this fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity to create lasting impact while advancing their professional development. Visit HERE to read more about the Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) 2026. Conclusion The Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) 2026 is an outstanding leadership and capacity-building opportunity for young women across the ECOWAS region. Through expert training, mentorship, project funding, policy engagement, and regional collaboration, the programme equips participants with the tools needed to become influential leaders addressing one of West Africa’s most urgent development challenges. If you are a young woman committed to advancing climate resilience, gender equality, and sustainable development, this fully funded fellowship offers the resources, support, and network to help you drive meaningful change in your community and beyond. Interested applicants should prepare their application materials and submit them before 19 July 2026 . Discover more global Fellowship , African , and Women opportunities on OFY. The post Women’s Fellowship on Human Mobility in the Context of Climate Change (HMCCC) 2026: Fully Funded ECOWAS Fellowship for Young Women Leaders Driving Climate Mobility Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA STAR-7 Program 2026: Fully Virtual & Free NASA Space Biology Research Training for Scientists Worldwide | Applications Open Until July 31
summary: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has officially opened applications for the 7th annual Spaceflight Technology, Applications, and Research (STAR-7) Program , a prestigious virtual professional development initiative designed to prepare researchers for conducting biological experiments in space. The STAR Program is one of NASA s flagship educational initiatives in the field of space biosciences, helping experienced researchers transition into space-based biological research by providing comprehensive training on designing, planning, and implementing experiments aboard NASA and commercial spaceflight platforms. Running from September 2026 through February 2027 , the STAR-7 Program offers participants direct exposure to NASA experts, experienced spaceflight investigators, technology developers, and commercial space providers—all without requiring travel. Most importantly, participation is completely free , making this an exceptional opportunity for qualified researchers around the world who aspire to contribute to the future of biological research in space. What is the NASA STAR Program? Spaceflight Technology, Applications, and Research (STAR) is an annual virtual NASA training programme dedicated to advancing the field of space biosciences. The programme is specifically designed to prepare experienced researchers for conducting biological experiments under spaceflight conditions. Participants gain practical knowledge about the scientific principles, operational requirements, technological tools, and logistical considerations necessary for successful research missions conducted aboard spacecraft and orbital platforms. Unlike traditional academic courses, STAR focuses on real-world applications of space biology, allowing participants to understand both the opportunities and challenges associated with conducting scientific investigations beyond Earth. Since its launch in 2020, the programme has trained multiple cohorts of researchers and has established itself as one of NASA s premier educational initiatives for future space bioscience investigators. Programme Objectives NASA developed the STAR Programme to achieve several important goals, including: Train principal investigators, senior researchers, and postdoctoral scholars in space biosciences. Introduce researchers to available NASA and commercial spaceflight research platforms. Educate participants on designing successful biological experiments for space environments. Increase awareness of available technologies and flight hardware. Foster collaborations between new researchers and experienced NASA-funded investigators. Build interdisciplinary research teams capable of advancing space biology. What Participants Will Learn Throughout the six-month virtual programme, participants receive comprehensive instruction covering numerous aspects of space biological research. Topics include: Fundamental principles of space biosciences Biology experiments conducted in microgravity Spaceflight hardware and research technologies Designing biological experiments for space missions Technical requirements for conducting spaceflight research Logistical planning for experiments aboard spacecraft Scientific opportunities available through NASA Commercial spaceflight research platforms Experimental limitations in space environments Flight mission preparation Collaboration strategies within the international space biology community The curriculum balances theoretical knowledge with practical insights from scientists who have successfully completed spaceflight experiments. Learn Directly from NASA Experts One of the most valuable aspects of the STAR Programme is direct interaction with professionals actively working within NASA s space biosciences ecosystem. Participants have opportunities to engage with: NASA programme scientists Experienced principal investigators Space biology researchers Technology developers Commercial flight providers Researchers who have previously conducted spaceflight experiments These interactions provide valuable insights into planning, funding, collaboration, and executing research missions in space. Build an International Research Network Beyond technical education, STAR places a strong emphasis on community building. Participants become part of a growing international network of researchers interested in biological sciences conducted in space. Networking opportunities include: Collaborating with researchers from different scientific disciplines Connecting with experienced NASA investigators Meeting commercial spaceflight partners Establishing future interdisciplinary research teams Learning about future funding opportunities Sharing ideas and research interests with peers worldwide This collaborative environment has become one of the programme s defining strengths. Who Can Apply? NASA welcomes applications from qualified individuals worldwide. Eligible applicants include researchers affiliated with: Universities Research institutions Commercial organizations NASA Centers Government agencies International organizations The programme particularly encourages participation from researchers affiliated with countries that are signatories to the Artemis Accords. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Hold an advanced degree (MD, PhD, or equivalent qualification). Be a Principal Investigator, Senior Research Scientist, or Postdoctoral Scholar. Postdoctoral applicants must include a letter of reference from their supervisor. The programme is not currently open to undergraduate or graduate students. NASA notes that future complementary programmes may be developed specifically for students. Course Format The STAR Programme has been intentionally designed to maximize accessibility for researchers across the globe. Programme features include: Fully virtual delivery No travel required Free participation International accessibility Interactive online sessions Expert-led lectures Networking opportunities Collaborative discussions Because the course is conducted entirely online, participants can engage from anywhere in the world. Course Timeline Applications Open: Currently Open Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 Participant Selection: August 2026 Course Duration: September 2026 – February 2027 Why Researchers Should Apply The STAR Programme offers several significant advantages for scientists seeking to enter the rapidly growing field of space biology. Benefits include: Free NASA professional training Direct learning from NASA experts Practical understanding of spaceflight experiments Exposure to cutting-edge biological research Networking with international researchers Collaboration opportunities with experienced investigators Insights into NASA research opportunities Understanding commercial space research platforms Preparation for future biological space missions Enhanced research career development Feedback from Previous Participants Researchers who have completed previous STAR cohorts consistently highlight the programme s educational value and collaborative environment. Participants have praised STAR for: Providing an excellent introduction to space biosciences. Helping researchers understand NASA s research ecosystem. Building interdisciplinary collaborations. Offering valuable networking opportunities. Explaining the practical realities of conducting experiments in space. Preparing scientists to participate in future spaceflight research. Many alumni now work within NASA programmes, universities, national laboratories, and leading research institutions. STAR Programme Organizers The programme is led by distinguished NASA experts. Dr. Ursula Koniges (NASA Headquarters) Deputy Program Scientist for Translational Research at NASA s Biological and Physical Sciences Division. Dr. Christina CJ Johnson (NASA Ames Research Center) Chief of Space Biosciences Research and Deputy Center Lead for the Human Research Program. Together, they oversee the STAR Programme and guide its scientific and educational direction. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Is the programme free? Yes. Participation is completely free. Is travel required? No. The programme is entirely virtual. Does NASA provide funding? No participant funding is provided because there are no travel or participation costs. However, participants learn about future funding and collaboration opportunities throughout the programme. Can undergraduate or graduate students apply? No. The programme currently targets Principal Investigators, Senior Researchers, and Postdoctoral Scholars. How are participants selected? Applications submitted through NASA s NSPIRES system are reviewed by the STAR organisation team according to NASA policies. Successful applicants will receive notification through NSPIRES. Why This Opportunity Matters As biological research increasingly expands beyond Earth s atmosphere, researchers with expertise in designing experiments for spaceflight are becoming more valuable than ever. NASA s STAR Programme provides an accessible pathway for experienced scientists to enter this exciting field by combining world-class instruction, expert mentorship, practical guidance, and global networking. For researchers interested in understanding how biology behaves in space and contributing to future scientific discoveries aboard orbital platforms and beyond, STAR-7 represents one of the most comprehensive professional development opportunities currently available. Learn More Apply Researchers meeting the eligibility requirements are strongly encouraged to submit their applications before 31 July 2026 . Click HERE to learn more and apply for the NASA STAR-7 Program 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post NASA STAR-7 Program 2026: Fully Virtual Free NASA Space Biology Research Training for Scientists Worldwide | Applications Open Until July 31 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Hedayah Careers 2026: Apply for Program Officer and Program Associate Jobs in Abu Dhabi, UAE
summary: Professionals seeking an opportunity to contribute to global peace, security, and resilience can now apply for exciting career opportunities at Hedayah , the International Center of Excellence for Countering Extremism and Violent Extremism. Hedayah is currently recruiting qualified and motivated professionals to join its international team based in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates (UAE) . The organization is hiring for two key positions: Program Officer Program Associate These positions offer an opportunity to work alongside governments, international organizations, researchers, practitioners, and community leaders to develop innovative solutions that help prevent and counter extremism and violent extremism around the world. Application Deadline: July 31, 2026 About Hedayah Hedayah is an internationally recognized center of excellence dedicated to preventing and countering violent extremism through collaboration, research, capacity building, and international partnerships. Working across multiple regions, Hedayah supports governments and institutions in strengthening resilience, promoting prevention strategies, and developing sustainable approaches to addressing evolving global security challenges. Its work focuses on empowering communities, strengthening institutions, and promoting evidence-based solutions that create long-term positive impact. Joining Hedayah means becoming part of an international organization committed to building safer, more resilient societies worldwide. Available Positions 1. Program Officer The Program Officer will play a leadership role in designing, managing, and implementing international programs that support national and regional efforts to prevent and counter extremism and violent extremism. Successful candidates will contribute to the development of innovative initiatives while overseeing complex international projects that engage a diverse range of stakeholders. Key Responsibilities The Program Officer will: Lead the design and implementation of international programs. Manage complex projects from planning through completion. Develop innovative capacity-building initiatives. Engage with governments and international partners. Coordinate with practitioners and technical experts. Deliver evidence-based programming. Support regional and international prevention efforts. Contribute to sustainable solutions that strengthen resilience. 2. Program Associate The Program Associate will support the implementation of Hedayah s international programs by coordinating activities and assisting project delivery. This position offers an excellent opportunity for professionals interested in international development, project coordination, and global partnerships. Key Responsibilities The Program Associate will: Support the planning and implementation of international projects. Coordinate project activities. Engage with partner organizations and stakeholders. Assist in delivering technical assistance initiatives. Support capacity-building programs. Work closely with the Programs team. Help ensure projects are delivered efficiently and effectively. Contribute to Hedayah s global mission of prevention and resilience. Why Work at Hedayah? Working at Hedayah provides professionals with the opportunity to: Build an international career in Abu Dhabi, UAE. Collaborate with global experts and policymakers. Work on projects with international impact. Contribute to evidence-based solutions addressing global challenges. Support innovative capacity-building initiatives. Engage with governments, international organizations, researchers, and practitioners. Help strengthen resilience and promote sustainable peace worldwide. Gain valuable experience in international program management and development. Who Should Apply? Hedayah is seeking talented, motivated, and mission-driven professionals who are passionate about creating positive change through international collaboration. Ideal candidates are individuals who: Enjoy working in multicultural and international environments. Have strong project coordination or program management skills. Are interested in international development and public policy. Value collaboration and stakeholder engagement. Want to contribute to safer and more resilient communities globally. Working Location Successful candidates will join Hedayah s international team in: Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates (UAE) Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: July 31, 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the closing date. How to Apply Applicants should visit Hedayah s official careers page to review the detailed job descriptions, eligibility requirements, and application instructions before submitting their application. Click here for more and to apply Prospective applicants should carefully read the responsibilities and qualifications for their preferred position and prepare all required application documents before applying. Why This Opportunity Stands Out Working with Hedayah offers more than just employment—it provides an opportunity to contribute to international initiatives that address some of today s most significant global challenges. Whether leading complex international programs as a Program Officer or supporting impactful projects as a Program Associate, successful candidates will play an important role in strengthening prevention efforts, advancing resilience, and supporting sustainable peace through global collaboration.
